name: Datamosh (Feedback Echo)
description: Reproducible twin of create_datamosh in feedback_echo mode: a source is combined with the previous output via a Composite TOP (operand 'maximum' so it stays bounded under feedback gain), a Feedback TOP re-injects the loop output, and a Level TOP whose brightness1 is the decay multiplier controls the trail length. A synthetic animated noiseTOP stands in as the source so it previews offline; swap it for a selectTOP to mosh real footage. The frame_blend / time_echo modes use Cache/TimeMachine TOPs instead. Offline-validated against RecipeSchema; the recursive look only fully develops after several cooked frames (live-cook-validated on TD 099 build 2025.32820; the recursive look develops over several cooked frames).

nodes.name: source_noise
nodes.comment: Synthetic animated color source — swap for a selectTOP / moviefileinTOP to mosh real content.

nodes.name: feedback1
nodes.comment: Holds the previous output frame; its target is wired to the decay node via the top parameter.

nodes.name: comp1
nodes.comment: Combines source (input 0) with the fed-back frame (input 1). 'maximum' stays bounded under feedback gain.

nodes.name: decay1
nodes.comment: Decay multiplier (brightness1) — sets how long the echo trails persist.

parameters.name: feedback_target
parameters.description: Feedback TOP samples the loop output (decay1). Value resolves to the created node path at build time.

preview_description: An animated noise field that smears into itself frame after frame, leaving bright motion echoes that fade by the decay amount — a datamosh feedback trail.
